# John Whitton

> John Whitton's personal website

import { ZoomImage } from "../public/components/ZoomImage";

<ZoomImage src="/images/JohnWhittonWorkHistory.png" alt="John Whitton Work History" title="John Whitton Work History" />

Hello, I'm John, and I'm a research engineer, technical leader, and entrepreneur working at the intersection of blockchain infrastructure (Solving, Market Making, Arbitrage) and Decentralized protocols (Intents, Liquidity Management, Settlement). I have a track record of driving projects from conception to completion, identifying research gaps, and providing solutions. Finally, I love leading engineering teams and have the ability to drive collaboration across the organization and the entire ecosystem.

I build high-performance solving and market-making infrastructure and DeFi protocols (Intents, Liquidity Management, Settlement), including

* Solver: Advanced Liquidity Mapping and Route Evaluation supporting multiple strategies for solving and market making.
  * Built on Tycho with its own liquidity mapping layer, able to evaluate over 1000 routes per second
* Solving Integration: UniswapX, 1inch, and CowSwap
* DeFi: UniswapV4 Hooks, NoLiquidity Solving, IntentSwapHook
* Liquidity Management: ERC-6909 Resource Management built on the compact

I use [jincubator](https://github.com/jincubator) for research and development of prototypes.

Below is a quick professional summary, more detail can be found on [the resume page](https://johnwhitton.com/resume/intro)

I worked as a Senior Protocol Engineer, previously Engineering Manager at [Eco](https://eco.com) building a payment focused intent protocol on a dedicated L3 rollup. Read the docs [here](https://docs.eco.com/) and the code [here](https://github.com/eco/eco-routes).

I have researched zero knowledge proofs, cryptographic primitives and consensus protocols.

I consulted as a research engineer providing strategy, research, design and building out DeFi infrastructure including trustless bridging, web3 identity and wallets. Deployments have been on EVM compatible chains including [Ethereum](https://ethereum.org/en/) and [Harmony](https://www.harmony.one/).

I prototyped the DEX [Kanga Finance](https://github.com/kangafinance) (inspired by [Sushi](https://github.com/sushiswap)) and a DeFi parachain [eave.network](https://github.com/eavenetwork) (inspired by [Acala](https://github.com/acalanetwork)). Deployments on [Harmony](https://www.harmony.one/) and [Polkadot](https://polkadot.network/). As founder for these projects, I was responsible for writing whitepapers, reviewing legal infrastructure, and fundraising. Although projects had investor interest and were awarded [hackathon prizes](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1ZGrbKSaAdtzvMzVh0EVFBfUvA4SqiAeYVXGhJN7Orbs/edit#slide=id.g48989ac23a_0_0) the projects did not move forward due to a combination of team commitment, market fit and crypto market conditions.

Previously I was as an Ecosystem Architect at Harmony. Where I helped launch the Harmony Mainnet, grew the ecosystem and was also intimately involved with hiring decisions and business strategy. Prior to this I was CTO for Loyyal (BaaS company) where we built private permissioned blockchains on Ethereum and Hypersphere Fabric and I managed a team of seven engineers.

I have worked extensively with decentralized financial protocols, bringing a unique perspective by combining my extensive corporate experience with IBM, SAP, Deloitte and KPMG with the disruptive financial models being developed on blockchain.

My earlier career focused on enterprise applications, service oriented architectures and cross application business processes. Where I founded a consultancy firm which grew to $800k in revenues and ran from 2000-2009. One of my former clients, EFI ($1.6 billion market cap) brought me on as a Manager in Enterprise Systems Architecture where I remained for 6 years.

I have been passionate about software and technology since high school. I graduated from the University of Queensland with a Bachelor of Science majoring in Computer Science before travelling globally leading the design and development of many Service Oriented Architectures and Blockchain implementations.

### Further Reading

* [The State of InterOp 2025](https://blog.li.fi/the-state-of-interop-2025-4a75d82a9bb9)
* [Intent Collective: a list of working projects in the Intents ecosystem](https://github.com/EnsoFinance/IntentCollective)
* [Illuminating Ethereum's Order Flow Landscape](https://writings.flashbots.net/illuminate-the-order-flow)

This is an example page.


## Get started

Hello world!


**Quick resumes:**

* [Engineering Resume (PDF)](https://johnwhitton.com/images/john.pdf)
* [Engineering Manager Resume (PDF)](https://johnwhitton.com/images/john_em.pdf)

### Resume (Quick Overview)

**Looking for:**

* Senior Engineer (Rust, Backend, Distributed Systems, Payments & Crypto Infrastructure)
* Engineering Manager / Technical Lead (Platform, Fintech, Distributed Systems, Developer Infrastructure)

### Professional Summary

Senior engineer, engineering leader, and entrepreneur specializing in distributed financial systems, payments infrastructure, blockchain-based platforms, and high-performance execution systems.

Over 15+ years, I have built and delivered production systems across enterprise platforms and decentralized networks, including real-time execution engines, cross-chain settlement infrastructure, stablecoin-based payment systems, developer platforms, and enterprise integration systems.

Recent work includes high-performance trading and routing infrastructure evaluating 1000+ execution paths per second, liquidity ingestion pipelines, and protocol design for programmable money and stablecoin payments on dedicated rollup infrastructure.

I focus on correctness, reliability, and system design under real-world constraints, including transaction processing, distributed coordination, secure APIs, cloud infrastructure, DevOps operations, and execution across fragmented liquidity and payment networks.

I have led engineering teams across the US, India, Africa, and fully remote environments, including teams of up to 20+ engineers and larger enterprise delivery organizations. Leadership experience includes recruiting, mentoring, performance management, Agile processes, engineering standards, partner success, and cross-functional execution.

Over the past decade, I have worked primarily with early-stage startups and emerging blockchain protocols, operating in highly ambiguous environments and driving projects from concept through production. I have founded multiple initiatives including EAVE (Polkadot parachain) and Global Enterprise Solutions.

#### Payments & Transaction Infrastructure

* Designed and operated systems processing on-chain transactions across blockchain networks (Harmony, Loyyal, Eco)
* Built infrastructure supporting transaction validation, execution, and settlement across distributed systems
* Integrated enterprise systems (EFI) with traditional payment rails, including credit card processing via SAP integrations
* Designed stablecoin-based payment systems and explored real-time payment orchestration across blockchain and off-chain systems
* Developed execution pipelines ensuring correctness, consistency, and reliability of financial transactions under real-world conditions

#### Security & Reliability

* Designed and implemented secure API layers with authentication and authorization (AuthN/AuthZ) for enterprise and blockchain systems
* Built permissioned API access models for corporate users (Loyyal) and developer platforms (Harmony)
* Implemented secure transaction processing with strong integrity and validation guarantees
* Experience with encryption, key management, and secure handling of financial and user data
* Designed systems with a security-first mindset, including API protection, access control, and operational monitoring

#### Cloud & DevOps

* Deployed and operated distributed backend systems across AWS and IBM Cloud (BlueMix)
* Built and managed CI/CD pipelines using Terraform for infrastructure provisioning and deployment
* Operated high-availability systems with 24/7 monitoring using PagerDuty and DataDog
* Scaled blockchain infrastructure:
  * Harmony: 300+ nodes deployed, 600+ external validators supported
  * Loyyal: private Ethereum (Geth) networks for enterprise deployments
* Managed production systems using MongoDB Atlas and AWS services
* Designed systems for reliability, uptime, and operational resilience

#### Engineering Leadership

* Led distributed engineering teams across the US, India, Africa (Kenya), and fully remote environments
* Owned hiring and recruiting for backend, frontend, DevOps, and blockchain engineering roles
* Conducted performance management including reviews, goal setting, and career development
* Implemented Agile processes, engineering standards, and team operating practices
* Built engineering cultures focused on ownership, delivery, and continuous improvement

#### Selected Systems

* **DeFi Arbitrager (Rust)**\
  Built low-latency execution system evaluating 1000+ routes/sec using live liquidity ingestion (Tycho / Substreams), performing pricing, profitability evaluation, and execution path construction across multiple venues.

* **Liquidity Mapping + Execution Layer**\
  Designed unified abstraction layer for liquidity discovery, normalization, and routing across fragmented DeFi protocols.

* **Cross-Chain Execution + Settlement**\
  Built systems for cross-chain routing and settlement, including batch execution across multiple environments.

* **Intent-Based Execution Systems**\
  Developed architecture for intent-based routing and settlement, including ERC-6909 resource management.

#### Key Achievements

* Built end-to-end execution pipeline: ingestion → mapping → pricing → execution
* Designed real-time routing and arbitrage infrastructure
* Developed transaction execution and settlement systems
* Delivered production systems across blockchain and payments infrastructure
* Led engineering teams up to 20+ developers

#### Experience (Summary)

**Founder / Research Engineer — Jincubator (2018–Present)**\
Built execution infrastructure, routing systems, stablecoin research, and cross-chain settlement pipelines.

**Engineering Manager / Senior Protocol Engineer — Eco (2023–2025)**\
Led team building L3 rollup infrastructure, stablecoin platform, and payment execution systems.

**Ecosystem Architect — Harmony (2019–2020)**\
Scaled team 6→20, launched mainnet and developer platform.

**CTO — Loyyal (2016–2018)**\
Built blockchain-based payment and enterprise infrastructure.

***

### Detailed Experience & Projects

Below are selected systems, achievements, and roles highlighting experience across trading infrastructure, payment systems, blockchain protocols, and distributed platforms.

### Projects / Work History

Below are links to projects I have designed and built or contributed to.

#### Founder / Research Engineer

##### [johnwhitton](https://johnwhitton.com): July 2018 - Present, Silicon Valley

* [jincubator.com](https://github.com/jincubator) (2025): High Performance Solving Infrastructure including
  * [Solver](https://www.jincubator.com/research/solving/solving): Route evaluation (over 1000 routes per second) using live streaming data (built on Tycho) with its own liquidity mapping layer, profitability calculator, and execution engine.
  * [Solving Prototyping](https://www.jincubator.com/research/solving/tycho1inchNOL): CowSwap, UniswapX and 1inch
  * [DeFi](https://www.jincubator.com/research/solving/intent-management-hook): Lending Markets, Leverage Tokens, UniswapV4, NoLiquidity Solving, IntentSwaps, Velora Integration
  * [Liquidity Management](https://www.jincubator.com/research/Intents/protocol): ERC-6909 Resource Management built on the compact

* [isolab.gg](https://github.com/isolab-gg/isolab.gg): Trustless bridge research using zero knowledge proofs including cryptographic primitives, consensus layer and zero knowledge implementations

* [hiddenstate.xyz](https://hiddenstate.xyz/): [1.country](https://github.com/harmony-one/1ns-docs) implementation inspired by [ENS](https://ens.domains/) with web2 DNS and SMTP integration

* [modulo](https://modulo.so/): non-custodial wallet and SMS-based crypto wallet

* [kanga.finance](https://kanga.finance/): DEX implementation and Harmony hackathon winner ($30k)

* [eave.network](https://eave.network/): Polkadot DeFi parachain

* em∆sset ([github](https://github.com/emasset)):
  * Designed stablecoin payment platform integrating with banking systems
  * Built gRPC API layer and mobile integrations

* WASDEX: decentralized regulated digital securities platform

* Shell Protocol: early prototype of stablecoin meta-layer

* Project X: Layer 1 blockchain whitepaper

* Pocketh: mobile stablecoin payments platform for restaurants

***

### Work History

#### Engineering Manager - Senior Protocol Engineer

##### [Eco](https://eco.com): Aug 2023 - Jan 2025, Silicon Valley

* Led a team of 8 engineers
* Built L3 rollup and stablecoin payment platform
* Developed intent-based cross-chain execution
* Managed bend.eco (stablecoin payments)
* Deployed AWS + MongoDB Atlas infrastructure
* Implemented CI/CD pipelines with Terraform
* Used PagerDuty and DataDog for monitoring and escalation
* Recruited engineers and managed performance reviews

#### Ecosystem Architect

##### [Harmony](https://www.harmony.one/): August 2019 - May 2020

* Hired and mentored 10+ engineers (team 6 → 20)
* Launched mainnet, token swap, and staking
* Deployed 300+ nodes and supported 600+ validators
* Implemented monitoring and DevOps systems
* Owned APIs, SDKs, and developer tooling
* Led partner success and developer adoption

#### Chief Technology Officer

##### [loyyal](https://loyyal.com/): July 2016 - Jun 2018

* Led team of 7 engineers across US and Africa
* Built enterprise blockchain payment systems
* Deployed private Ethereum + IBM Cloud infrastructure
* Built Dubai Points mobile application
* Managed enterprise integrations (IBM, Deloitte, Emirates, CIBC)

#### Founder / Engineering Manager

Enterprise Software and Consulting: 1986 - 2016

* Managed 100+ initiatives and teams of up to 50 engineers
* Built SOA platforms integrating SAP, Salesforce, and external systems
* Integrated SAP with credit card processing systems
* Founded and scaled consulting firm to $800k revenue

***

<object data="/images/john.pdf" width="1000" height="1000" type="application/pdf" />


#### [Andy](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andy-wu-4329664/)

John is one of the most professional engineers I’ve ever worked with. He takes the ownership of many OPS works,
and documents them down with meticulous details. Also, he is a fast learner, and eager to pick up new skills. He also has very good project management skills, and I believe he could be a very good leader either in biz or eng departments.


#### [Ganesha](https://www.linkedin.com/in/gupadhyaya/)

John is dedicated, hard-working, great work ethics, and brings lot of positive energy. no doubt that he is a great manager with good people skills. john led the whole hiring process and single handedly managed it well, without much chaos. no joke how much time and energy it takes. great job. while i believe it was not the best use of john's time, he embraced whatever this staking launch demanded from him. great work towards managing the release (deployment, documentation, etc) and taking some load of andy and daniel. after the staking release, john could refocus and quickly get back to speed on the ecosystem/tooling/partner tasks/goals for q4 and expedite some of the deliverables.


import { ZoomImage } from "../../public/components/ZoomImage";

## John's Colleagues

<ZoomImage src="/images/HarmonyTeam.jpg" alt="Harmony Team" title="HarmonyTeam" />

I have been fortunate over my long career to have the opportunity to work with some of the most talented individuals and teams not only in Silicon Valley, but globally.

In my role as a founder, cto, ecoystem architect and engineer. I have learned from great leaders,
researchers, business strategists and engineers. I have built great teams and mentored many talented junior engineers. I have watched my teams apply their solid technical foundation to become invaluable contributors to the team and the organization. Watching individuals grow both personally and professionally is the most rewarding work I have done.

Here is a video highlighting the strengths of the Harmony Team that I was fortunate enough to work with.

#### [Janet](https://www.linkedin.com/in/janet-liang-754090135/)

John is always willing to work on whatever the team needs even though you don't necessarily want to.
thank you for listening and being a great mentor.


#### [Li](https://www.linkedin.com/in/lijiang2087/)

I want to thank John for having a detailed process and documentation of the OSTN network upgrades and resets and communicating to me and community.


#### [Matt](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mattlockyer/)

John is amazing at explaining and delegating. I’m always impressed with his knowledge base. I think more efforts could be made to capture his knowledge in “explainers”, that would go a long way to supporting the community. John can also run anything, like up and running in a matter of minutes. This means he can troubleshoot tons of deployment issues from the core protocol all the way up to the application layer


#### [Nick](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nickwh8te/)

John is always willing to jump on whatever needs to get done. he jumped SDK to hiring and then back into network ops. I think that John does a good job of recruiting and onboarding new interns and giving them appropriate difficulty tasks as they ramp up. I'm grateful that he stepped in to cover miami devcon for me at the last minute. team player


#### [Olivier](https://www.linkedin.com/in/olivierveyrac/)

John and I worked together at Autodesk during the implementation of the subscription program, John was a lead SAP developer working on the program. I frequently interacted with him as we rolled out the program. John has the rare gift of being able to understand complex business problems and map them to a technical solution. His drive and passion for the project, combined with his excellent communication skills and deep technical knowledge, were key factors in the successful rollout of the program. We've since become close friends. I'd recommend anyone looking for a technical lead with business acumen to consider John.


#### [Yishuang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/chenyishuang/)

John is a really good team player, among his good qualities, I admire three most.

1. Helpful and warm-hearted whenever you ask for help, John will give you the best help.
2. Avid learner, John likes to learn new things and will actively take new tasks. I see John actively in ops recently.
3. Responsible, longer view, John focused a lot on knowledge transfer. He wants to make sure the knowledge can be shared with the whole team and future new employees, so he contributed a lot on internal wiki gitbooks, which I think it’s a good point.
